chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.706 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.3784/2026 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.3785/2026 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ----------------------------------------------------- 04.03.2026 Mr. Ilyas Ahmed Awan, advocate a/w applicants ------------------------------------ 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. Applicants Amir Sohail son of Muhammad Ibrahim, Muhammad Zeeshan son of Muhammad Ibrahim, Asif Khan Yousuf Zai son of Gul Muhammad and Mohammad Jan sonof Tasir Jan seek pre-arrest bail in FIR No.139/2025 registered at Police Station Ahmed Pur, District Khairpur for offence under Section 506/2, 420, 148, 149, PPC. Learned counsel for applicants submits that the applicants/accused are innocent and they have been falsely implicated in this case due to mala fide intentions and ulterior motives; that they are law abiding citizens and intend to surrender before the concerned Court but they apprehend their arrest at the hands of police in order to humiliate and disgrace, hence they need protection in order to enable them to appear before the concerned Court. Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named, for a period of seven days from today, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants are directed to surrender before the concerned Court in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith.

Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named, for a period of seven days from today, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants are directed to surrender before the concerned Court in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of seven days from today or whenever the applicants surrender before the concerned Court, which one occurs earlier. The applicants are cautioned that this concession of protective bail will not be extended for any reason whatsoever and if they fail to surrender themselves before the relevant Court during validity of this protective bail, their bail bond shall stand forfeited. Instant Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of in the foregoing terms. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
